bids.reasoning: At the opening time t=0.0, I am targeting maximum utility and have no opponent offers or messages to infer their priorities. With no time pressure yet, it is appropriate to start with my top value on every issue and wait for the opponent’s counteroffer before conceding.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation, and the opponent’s only offer was the minimum-value package for me with no sign of meaningful concession yet. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid, I make a small concession on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is extremely early (t=0.04) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA offer that is very poor for me. I will make only a small concession on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D and the moderately important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not shown any real concession, repeatedly offering my lowest-valued bundle. Since issueA has the lowest weight for me, I am conceding there first while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am targeting a high-utility bid by conceding only on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding issueB/issueC/issueD at my best valueF. The opponent has repeatedly offered the all-valueA bid with no concession, and since it is still very early at t=0.08, there is no need to make costly concessions on my high-importance issues yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 by conceding only on the lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D. It is still very early at t=0.10, and the opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no observable concession, so I should not make major concessions yet.

I am targeting about 0.81 utility because it is still early, and the opponent has only made a small concession so far by moving issueC from valueA to valueB. Since they are mostly holding firm at very low-utility bids for me, I will not concede much on my high-weight issues, but I will make a small movement from my previous bid by offering issueD: valueE instead of valueF while keeping the rest strong.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 rather than moving close to the opponent’s low offers. The opponent has mostly held firm at all valueA, with only a small concession on issueC to valueB, so I will keep my most important issues strong while making a modest non-repeating concession on issueC.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a modest additional concession. The opponent has mostly held firm at valueA across issues, with only a small move to issueC:valueB, so I should not overreact; I will concede on lower-weight issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 while making a visible concession because the opponent has held mostly firm and only improved issueC from valueA to valueB. Since time is still early at 0.18, I should not drop close to their low-utility offer, but I can concede on lower/medium-cost areas while holding issueD, one of my most important issues, at its best value.

I am targeting a still-strong early-game utility around 0.76, since at t=0.20 there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has repeatedly held issueA/issueB/issueD at valueA but recently conceded issueC up to valueD, so I incorporate their latest movement on issueC while holding firm on higher-value positions elsewhere.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because we are only at t=0.22 and the opponent’s offers remain very low-value to me, with only minor movement on issueA and issueC. I am making a modest concession on issueD from my top value while keeping issueB strong, since issueD/valueE is nearly as good for me and may create room without giving up too much.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 because it is early-mid negotiation at t=0.24, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held at very low values for issueA/issueB/issueD but has started conceding on issueC, so I will concede the low-weight issueA fully and match their recent issueC movement while protecting high-importance issueD and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 because it is only early-mid negotiation time, so I should not chase the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent has made only small concessions from all-valueA, recently moving on issueB and earlier on issueC, while issueD remains fixed at valueA from them; I will concede on low-cost/low-weight areas and preserve strong outcomes on the most important issues, especially issueD.

I am targeting a strong but slightly more conciliatory utility around 0.74, since we are still before the midpoint and the opponent’s offers remain quite low for me. The opponent has mostly held firm on low values but has shown small movement on issueC and issueD, so I am conceding somewhat on issueD while keeping issueB high and maintaining issueC at a value they have previously offered.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.69 while making a meaningful concession on issueB, where the opponent’s latest offer moved up to valueD. The opponent has mostly held very low offers for me, with only small single-issue concessions, so I am preserving stronger outcomes on my high-weight issues C and D while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.65, making a moderate mid-game concession while preserving value on the high-weight issues C and D. The opponent’s valid offers remain quite low for me, though they have shown occasional movement on issueC and most recently issueD, so I am offering valueD on issueD while keeping issueC at valueC. With no messages to consider and time still only at 0.32, I am not yet dropping to their very low bids.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid-to-high range because t=0.34 is not yet late, but I should continue conceding on lower-weight issues. The opponent has mostly held firm near valueA, with small concessions on issueA/issueC/issueB/issueD, so I am offering movement on issueA and issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD reasonably well.

I am targeting a mid-utility bid around 0.64 for me, conceding modestly because we are still before the halfway point but the opponent has begun making small concessions. Their offers remain low for my utility, but recent bids show movement on issueB and issueC, so I concede on lower-weight issueA/issueB while protecting the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a moderate weighted utility around 0.64, which is a meaningful concession from my opening but still protects my high-weight issues. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure to explore tradeoffs, but it is not yet late enough to accept the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent has slowly signaled willingness on issueC and occasionally issueB/issueD, so I keep issueC at valueC and issueD at valueD while conceding issueA fully.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65, continuing to concede but not dropping near the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent has made only small concessions, mostly on issueA/issueB and occasionally issueC/issueD, so I concede on the lower-weight issues while holding moderate-good values on the high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.58, conceding fully on the lower-weight issues A and B while preserving value on the more important C and D. The opponent’s latest valid offers have improved for me, especially on C and D, but they still anchor hard on valueA for A/B, so I am responding with a tradeoff that gives them those issues while keeping D at a level they have previously signaled. There are no opponent messages to weigh beyond the offer pattern.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.52 by holding issueC at valueC, which is valuable to me and has appeared in the opponent’s offers, while conceding issueA and issueB to valueA. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their low bid outright; the opponent has slowly moved away from all-valueA offers, especially on issueC and occasionally issueD, so I am testing a compromise with issueD at valueC. There are no opponent messages to weigh, so I am relying only on the offer pattern.
